topic.name: Oil and Gas Regulation
topic.description: Legislation focused on the management and taxation of oil and gas extraction from the outer continental shelf to protect the environment and ensure fair returns to the United States.

Document 1: Amends the Outer Continental Shelf Lands Act
Increases the minimum royalty under such leases from 12.5% to 20%

topic.name: Tax Incentives and Benefits
topic.description: This cluster focuses on various tax incentives and benefits provided through amendments to the Internal Revenue Code for specific groups, including employee ownership, military personnel, and tax-exempt organizations.

Document 1: extends provisions allowing deferral of tax on gain from the sale of employer securities
allows a tax deduction for 50% of the interest incurred on loans to S corporation-sponsored ESOPs
Document 2: provides tax benefits and incentives for military personnel
makes permanent the election to treat combat zone compensation as earned income
Document 3: allows certain support organizations for tax-exempt teaching hospitals to acquire debt secured by income-producing real property

topic.name: Support for Underrepresented Students
topic.description: Legislative measures aimed at increasing educational access and success for underrepresented groups in STEM and other educational contexts.

Document 1: increase the participation of women and underrepresented minorities in the fields of science, technology, engineering, and mathematics (STEM)
mentoring programs that partner STEM professionals with students
Document 2: promote success for homeless youth by holding them to the same achievement standards as other students
require states, LEAs, and schools to promote success for homeless youth
Document 3: preventing federal student loan delinquency and default at IHEs that have a high cohort default rate
increasing the number of borrowers who successfully rehabilitate defaulted loans

topic.name: Medicare Policy Amendments
topic.description: Legislative changes related to the administration and coverage of services and equipment under Medicare.

Document 1: Amends title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act to treat as a physician, for Medicare purposes, a clinical psychologist acting within the scope of a psychologist's license.
with respect to the furnishing of clinical psychologists services which the psychologist is legally authorized to perform by the state.
Document 2: Amends title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act to permit a Medicare beneficiary to elect to take, or decline to take, ownership of a Group 3 Support Surface item of complex durable medical equipment (DME).
after the 13-month capped rental period ends.
Document 3: Medicare Prescription Drug Program Integrity and Transparency Act of 2013 - Amends part D (Voluntary Prescription Drug Benefit Program) of title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act.

to require each contract entered into with a prescription drug plan (PDP) sponsor with respect to a PDP the sponsor offers to prohibit the PDP from entering into a contract with any pharmacy benefits manager (PBM).

topic.name: Tariff Schedule Amendments
topic.description: The documents discuss amendments to the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States regarding the temporary suspension of duties on specific products.

Document 1: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to extend the temporary suspension of duty on strontium magnesium phosphate-tin doped inorganic products.
temporary suspension of duty
Document 2: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to suspend temporarily the duty on certain acrylic staple fibers.
suspend temporarily the duty on certain acrylic staple fibers
Document 3: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to suspend temporarily the duty on 2,4-Diamino-3-Y4-(2-sulfoxyethylsulfonyl)-phenylazo?-5-Y4-(2 sulfoxyethyl sulfonyl)-2-sulfophenylazo?-benzenesulfonic acid potassium sodium salt.
suspend temporarily the duty on 2,4-Diamino-3-Y4-(2-sulfoxyethylsulfonyl)-phenylazo?-5-Y4-(2 sulfoxyethyl sulfonyl)-2-sulfophenylazo?-benzenesulfonic acid potassium sodium salt

topic.name: Veterans Services Legislation
topic.description: Legislation focused on improving services and support for veterans through various programs and funding mechanisms.

Document 1: Authorizes the Secretary of Veterans Affairs (VA) to accept voluntary services performed by a veteran or veterans organization at a national cemetery.
Includes sharing the story of the veteran's military service.
Document 2: Reduces the training period for veterans' outreach program specialists from three years to one year.
Aims to enhance the effectiveness of veteran employment services.
Document 3: Directs the Secretary of the Treasury to allocate funds for veterans health care programs.
Ensures financial support for the functions and activities of the Veterans Health Administration.

topic.name: Legislative Actions on Social Issues
topic.description: This cluster encompasses various legislative measures aimed at addressing social issues such as terrorism, welfare support, and domestic violence.

Document 1: authorizes additional FY2017 appropriations to the Department of Justice for counter terrorism

broadens the scope of the prohibition to bar the transfer or receipt of a firearm knowing or reasonably believing that the firearm will be used to commit a crime of violence, a drug trafficking crime, or a federal crime of terrorism.
Document 2: removes the limitation on payments by the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS) to Puerto Rico, the Virgin Islands, Guam, and American Samoa under grants for assistance
makes such territories eligible for the TANF Contingency Fund for State Welfare Programs.
Document 3: establishes in the Department of Defense (DOD) an Office of the Victims' Advocate to facilitate access to services for victims of domestic or family violence
requires DOD policies for victim assistance, family advocacy, and equal opportunity programs to provide for a victims' advocates program within each military department.

topic.name: Mortgage Regulation Amendments
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative amendments related to the Truth in Lending Act and other financial regulations affecting mortgage origination and servicing.

Document 1: Amends the Truth in Lending Act to require each servicer of a home mortgage to respond in writing within 30 days to a mortgagor...
Authorizes an aggrieved individual to bring a civil action for damages...
Document 2: Amends the Truth in Lending Act to modify the exclusion from the definition of 'mortgage originator'...
Document 3: Amends the Truth in Lending Act (TILA) to require the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau (CFPB) to exempt from requirements...
Includes as a qualified mortgage...

topic.name: Indian Trust Asset Management
topic.description: This topic encompasses the federal government's fiduciary responsibilities and management of trust assets for Indian tribes, including the establishment of management plans and processes for real property transactions.

Document 1: reaffirms the federal government's fiduciary responsibilities to Indians
Department of the Interior must establish an Indian trust asset management demonstration project
Document 2: allows the Saginaw Chippewa Indian Tribe to transfer, lease, encumber, or otherwise convey interest in real property
bars the United States from being held liable for any loss resulting from the term of any transfer, lease, encumbrance, or conveyance of land

topic.name: Campaign Finance Transparency
topic.description: Legislation aimed at enhancing transparency in campaign finance and candidate financial disclosures.

Document 1: Amends the Federal Election Campaign Act of 1971 to require reporting of contributions of $1,000 or more.
Reporting requirements for contributions received by members of the Joint Select Committee on Deficit Reduction.
Document 2: Amends the Federal Election Campaign Act of 1971 to require presidential candidates to file income tax returns with the FEC.
Candidates must provide tax returns for the three most recent taxable years prior to nomination.
Document 3: Amends the Federal Election Campaign Act of 1971 to revise notification requirements for campaign contributions.
Requires Senate candidates to file reports directly with the FEC.

topic.name: U.S. Immigration Legislation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on various legislative acts aimed at reforming and regulating immigration processes and policies in the United States.

Document 1: Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to direct the Secretary of State to establish a pilot fee-based premium processing service to expedite visa interview appointments.
Revises the visa waiver program to authorize the Secretary of Homeland Security to designate any country as a program country.
Document 2: Amends the Illegal Immigration Reform and Immigrant Responsibility Act of 1996 to revise deadline and funding provisions for certain fencing and security project requirements along the southern border.
Document 3: Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to establish an annual cap on H-1B nonimmigrant visas at between 115,000 and 195,000 visas depending upon market conditions.
Directs the Secretary of Homeland Security to authorize the accompanying spouse of an H-1B alien to work in the United States.

topic.name: U.S. Military and Intelligence Security Legislation
topic.description: This cluster discusses various legislative measures aimed at enhancing the security and intelligence operations related to U.S. military personnel and national defense.

Document 1: Amends the National Security Act of 1947 to direct the head of an element of the intelligence community to provide intelligence information to the Secretary of Defense.
Requires the Secretary to report annually to Congress concerning the instances in which such information was provided.
Document 2: Authorizes appropriations for the conduct of intelligence and intelligence-related activities of various intelligence agencies.
Specifies authorized personnel ceilings for intelligence activities.
Document 3: Directs the Department of Defense to submit to Congress a plan to enhance security of military personnel at DOD installations.
Requires implementation of the plan recommendations within 90 days after enactment.

topic.name: FDA Regulatory Processes
topic.description: This topic encompasses various legislative measures aimed at streamlining and ensuring the integrity of the FDA's approval processes for drugs, devices, and biological products.

Document 1: establish a reciprocal marketing approval process
the FDA must grant or decline reciprocal marketing approval not later than 30 days after receiving a request.
Document 2: collect a user fee from each manufacturer
ensure correction of a violation of a Good Manufacturing Practices requirement
Document 3: applies debarment provisions currently applicable to abbreviated new drugs
Requires the Secretary of Health and Human Services to consider whether debarment will affect the public health

topic.name: Mental Health Legislation
topic.description: Legislation focused on improving mental health services and support systems for individuals with mental illness and substance abuse issues.

Document 1: amends the Public Health Service Act to revise and extend Projects for Assistance in Transition from Homelessness
provide services to individuals suffering from serious mental illness and substance abuse
Document 2: authorizes the use of specified funds for mental health services and substance abuse prevention
includes housing counseling and financial counseling for individuals with mental illness
Document 3: amends the Public Health Service Act to revise the authorities of the Substance Abuse and Mental Health Services Administration
establishes new requirements for SAMHSA to improve mental and substance use disorder services

topic.name: Agricultural Policy Initiatives
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative acts aimed at enhancing agricultural production, sustainability, and access to food resources in various contexts including urban settings and educational institutions.

Document 1: Amends the Farm Security and Rural Investment Act of 2002 to direct the Secretary of Agriculture to designate as bioenergy cropsheds areas that are physically located within a 50-mile radius, or some other economically-practicable distance, from an existing or planned biorefinery.
States that each designated bioenergy cropshed shall include local conservation objectives, including water quality, wildlife habitat, soil quality, and air quality.
Document 2: Requires the Secretary of Agriculture (USDA) to establish an urban agriculture outreach program to award grants to eligible entities for: (1) infrastructure, (2) land acquisition and conversion, (3) education and training, (4) assistance to farmers or ranchers in urban areas, and (5) other activities that support urban agricultural production.
Requires the USDA to award grants for scientific research on promoting and enhancing agricultural production in urban areas.
Document 3: Directs the Secretary of Agriculture to issue loan guarantees to local educational agencies, tribal organizations, or consortia of such entities to finance the construction, remodeling, or expansion of infrastructure or the purchase of durable equipment that will facilitate their provision of healthy meals through the school breakfast and lunch programs.
Favors loan guarantee applicants that demonstrate a substantial or disproportionate need (as compared with other applicants) for food service infrastructure or durable equipment.

topic.name: Iran Sanctions Legislation
topic.description: The documents discuss various U.S. legislative measures aimed at imposing sanctions on Iran due to its nuclear program and terrorism-related activities.

Document 1: prohibits the President from limiting the application of specified sanctions on Iran
relates to a claim brought against Iran or its political subdivisions, agencies, or instrumentalities
Document 2: changes the current discretionary authority to a requirement that the President impose sanctions
Sanctions are expanded to include visa denial and exclusion from the United States
Document 3: expresses the sense of Congress that the United States should use diplomatic and economic measures
subjects Iran to certain economic sanctions

topic.name: Pension and Retirement Legislation
topic.description: The documents discuss various legislative amendments related to employee benefit plans and pension regulations, particularly focusing on tribal laws and military survivor benefits.

Document 1: amends the Internal Revenue Code and the Employee Retirement Income Security Act of 1974 (ERISA) to treat employee benefit or pension plans maintained by Indian tribes
in the same manner as plans maintained by states
Document 2: Amends the Pension Protection Act of 2006 to make technical corrections to special funding rules applicable to pension plans
provide for plans with plan years beginning on the first day of the month
Document 3: PROTECT Act or Protecting the Retirement of Our Troops by Ensuring Compensation is Timely Act
prohibits the Secretary of a military department, regarding a participant in the Survivor Benefit Plan... from making a determination that the participant is missing

topic.name: Airline Regulation and Transparency
topic.description: This topic encompasses regulations and practices aimed at enhancing transparency and accountability in airline operations, including ticket pricing and performance reporting.

Document 1: revise certain airline service quality performance reporting regulations
disclose the nature and source of flight delays and cancellations
Document 2: unfair or deceptive practice for air carriers to display ticket prices without all applicable fees
require the Secretary of Transportation to issue regulations to establish a procedure to calculate fuel surcharges

topic.name: Fisheries Management and Conservation
topic.description: Legislation focused on the management, conservation, and protection of fishery resources and marine ecosystems.

Document 1: Amends the Magnuson-Stevens Fishery Conservation and Management Reauthorization Act
requires a stock survey and stock assessment of overfished stocks
Document 2: Amends the Magnuson-Stevens Fishery Conservation and Management Act
requires the payment of costs, fees, and expenses incurred by certain prevailing parties
Document 3: Amends the Coral Reef Conservation Act of 2000
authorizes actions to minimize injury to a coral reef or loss of an ecosystem function

topic.name: Labor Rights Legislation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislation aimed at protecting employee rights and promoting fair labor practices.

Document 1: Prohibits employment discrimination on the basis of actual or perceived sexual orientation or gender identity
Provides for enforcement, including giving the EEOC... the same enforcement powers as they have under specified provisions of the Civil Rights Act of 1964
Document 2: Amends the National Labor Relations Act to make it an unfair labor practice for: an employer to recognize or bargain collectively with a labor organization that has not been selected by a majority of the employees
Requires the NLRB to conduct a secret ballot election to determine whether a labor organization... is no longer the representative of a unit
Document 3: Amends the National Labor Relations Act to require the National Labor Relations Board to certify, without an election, an individual or labor organization to be the exclusive representative of the employees
The Federal Mediation and Conciliation Service (FMCS) shall use its best efforts, by mediation and conciliation, to bring the parties to agreement if 90 days lapse without settlement of a dispute

topic.name: Regulation of Digital Communications
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at regulating the taxation and provision of digital goods, services, and communications to ensure fairness and accessibility.

Document 1: Prohibits a state or local jurisdiction from imposing multiple or discriminatory taxes on or with respect to the sale or use of digital goods or services
Grants jurisdiction to federal district courts to prevent a violation of this Act
Document 2: Requires an intermediate provider that offers the capability to transmit certain voice communications to register with the Federal Communications Commission (FCC)
Prohibits certain long-distance voice service providers from using an intermediate provider to transmit voice communications unless the intermediate provider is so registered
Document 3: Allows multichannel video programming distributors to provide subscribers with any channel of video programming on an a la carte basis
Amends the Communications Act of 1934 to modify the types of programming constituting the minimum contents of basic tier service

topic.name: Data Privacy and Protection Legislation
topic.description: Legislation focused on the protection of personally identifiable information and the security measures required for handling such data.

Document 1: Defines 'personally identifiable information' as any information about an individual maintained by a federal agency
establishing minimum requirements for the protection of personally identifiable information maintained in or transmitted by mobile digital devices
Document 2: permits a video tape service provider to obtain a consumer's consent, which is required to disclose personally identifiable information concerning the consumer
Document 3: prohibit a provider of commercial mobile or commercial mobile data service from providing service on a mobile electronic device reported to such provider as stolen
Directs providers to make available to account holders the capability of deleting, from a remote location, all information that was placed on such a device after its manufacture

topic.name: Wildlife Conservation Legislation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on U.S. legislative acts aimed at regulating wildlife protection and management, particularly concerning migratory birds and endangered species.

Document 1: Amends the Migratory Bird Treaty Act to permit the taking of any migratory game bird
contains a standing crop (including an aquatic crop)
Document 2: Directs the Secretary of the Interior to issue a final decision on whether or not to issue a permit under the Endangered Species Act
permits construction of an elementary school in San Diego, California
Document 3: Grants the Secretary of the Interior permanent authority to authorize any state to issue electronic duck stamps
sets forth state electronic duck stamp application requirements

topic.name: Support for Small Businesses
topic.description: Legislative measures aimed at fostering small business growth and addressing regulatory impacts.

Document 1: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to allow a new business-related tax credit for 30% of the equity investment in a small business concern.
Requires the Secretary of the Treasury to provide data on the implementation and use of the equity investment in small business tax credit.
Document 2: Directs the Administrator of the Small Business Administration (SBA) to conduct an annual study of the total costs to small businesses of federal regulations.
Expresses the sense of Congress that no additional funds should be made available to the SBA to carry out this Act.
Document 3: Amends the Workforce Investment Act of 1998 to authorize the Secretary of Labor to award competitive grants to local boards, community colleges, and postsecondary vocational institutions.
Promote local economic growth and eliminate gaps between the workforce skills available and the workforce skills needed with respect to small businesses.

topic.name: Designation of Government Facilities
topic.description: This topic encompasses the formal naming and designation of various government buildings and wilderness areas in the United States.

Document 1: designates the facility of the United States Postal Service
as the 'Gale W. McGee Post Office'
Document 2: Directs the Administrator of General Services to ensure
is known and designated as the 'Ralph Regula Federal Office Building and Courthouse'
Document 3: Designates specified lands in Maine
to be known as the Cross Island Wilderness

topic.name: Election Integrity Legislation
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at ensuring the integrity, security, and accessibility of elections in the United States.

Document 1: Amends the Federal Election Campaign Act of 1971 to apply the ban on contributions and expenditures by foreign nationals
to domestic corporations whose shareholders include one or more foreign principals.
Document 2: Requires each voting system purchased after December 31, 2012, to produce an individual permanent paper record for each ballot that is cast.
to enhance accessibility and provide independence for persons with disabilities during the voting process.
Document 3: Requires states to hold special elections when a vacancy occurs in the office of U.S. Senator for the state.
to reimburse the states for a portion of special election costs.

topic.name: U.S. Trade and Tariff Legislation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on U.S. legislative measures related to tariffs, trade regulations, and economic policies affecting international commerce.

Document 1: Amends the Tariff Act of 1930 to make eligible for the drawback (refund) of duties, paid on certain imported merchandise
certain other merchandise classified as commercially interchangeable with such imported merchandise
Document 2: Cuban-American Family Rights Restoration Act - Prohibits that President from regulating or prohibiting: (1) travel to or from Cuba by any U.S. person
transactions incident to travel for the purpose of visiting a close relative who is a national of Cuba.
Document 3: Fair Currency Act of 2007 - Amends the Tariff Act of 1930 to expand the authority of the administering authority or the International Trade Commission
Includes exchange-rate misalignment (undervaluation of a foreign currency) by a country as a countervailable subsidy.

topic.name: Native Hawaiian and U.S. Territorial Governance
topic.description: This cluster addresses legislation related to the governance and health care initiatives for Native Hawaiians and U.S. territories.

1: Reauthorizes the Native Hawaiian Health Care Improvement Act.
Requires Papa Ola Lokahi to report on health care financing mechanisms.
2: Directs the Secretary of the Interior to ensure state flags encircle the Washington Monument.
3: Requires the Governor of the Virgin Islands to appoint a Chief Financial Officer.
Establishes the Virgin Islands Chief Financial Officer Search Commission.

topic.name: Volunteer Service and Scholarships
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation related to volunteer service initiatives and the provision of scholarships for individuals engaged in such service, particularly for seniors.

Document 1: Amends the National and Community Service Act of 1990 to authorize the Corporation for National and Community Service to award grants to public agencies or private nonprofit organizations experienced in administering service programs to provide Silver Scholarships to individuals who perform, over a 12-month period, at least 500 hours of volunteer service after attaining age 55.
Allows the proration of such scholarships when an individual, due to compelling personal circumstances, cannot perform 500, but performs at least 250, hours of service within the 12-month period.
Document 2: Amends the National and Community Service Act of 1990 to authorize the Corporation for National and Community Service to award grants to public agencies or private nonprofit organizations experienced in administering service programs to provide Silver Scholarships to individuals who perform, over a 12-month period, at least 500 hours of volunteer service after attaining age 55.
Allows a scholarship recipient to designate another individual to use such scholarship.

topic.name: Postal Service Reform
topic.description: Legislation aimed at modernizing and improving the operations, services, and financial sustainability of the U.S. Postal Service.

Document 1: Improving Postal Operations, Service, and Transparency Act of 2015
restructuring the pre-funding requirements for USPS retiree health benefits
Document 2: Reform the Postal Service for the 21st Century Act
requires the Postal Service to implement a plan to replace or convert, by January 1, 2017, 2,000 gasoline-powered delivery vehicles with electric vehicles

topic.name: Environmental and Health Regulations
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at protecting natural resources and public health through regulatory measures.

Document 1: Withdraws federal lands within the Rainy River Drainage Basin in Minnesota from all forms of entry, appropriation, or disposal under the public land laws.
Requires an environmental impact statement conducted for such an action to include consideration of any potential impacts to units of the National Park System.
Document 2: Requires federal agencies to report to Congress on any activity to use or develop data mining to identify terrorist or criminal activity.
Includes an assessment of the efficacy of the activity in providing accurate information and the impact on individual privacy and civil liberties.
Document 3: Establish the National Pulmonary Fibrosis Advisory Board; develop a system to collect data on pulmonary fibrosis and other interstitial lung diseases.
Focus on strategies to increase public education and awareness of pulmonary fibrosis.

topic.name: Legislative Acts for Commemoration and Support
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative acts aimed at recognizing historical events, supporting specific communities, and promoting social enterprises.

Document 1: Establishes the Commission on the Advancement of Social Enterprise
identify opportunities for the federal government to engage social enterprises
Document 2: Authorizes the Secretary of the Army to place in Arlington National Cemetery an American Braille tactile flag
honoring blind members of the Armed Forces
Document 3: Directs the Secretary of the Interior to establish a Civil War Sesquicentennial Commemoration Commission
to plan, develop, and carry out programs and activities appropriate to commemorate the sesquicentennial of the Civil War

topic.name: Healthcare and Safety Regulations
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at improving safety and transparency in healthcare and mining industries.

Document 1: require each participating hospital to adopt and implement a staffing system
provide for the public reporting of certain staffing information
Document 2: establish an independent National Mine Safety Board
requires mine inspections to be conducted during various shifts and days of the week
Document 3: require hospitals and ambulatory surgical centers to report to the Department of Health and Human Services
HHS must publish on its website such information in a manner that promotes charge comparisons

topic.name: Government Policy and Declassification
topic.description: The documents discuss government policies related to information classification, unemployment assistance, and military retirement benefits.

Document 1: Directs federal agency heads with authority to classify information to specify the types of information with short-lived sensitivity that could be automatically declassified without further review.

Requires federal agency heads that classify information to report to Congress on options for pilot programs to assess the feasibility and advisability of mechanisms to improve the current declassification capabilities of federal agencies.
Document 2: Amends title XII (Advances to State Unemployment Fund) of the Social Security Act, as added by the Assistance for Unemployed Workers and Struggling Families Act, to extend through December 31, 2012, the waiver of certain interest payments on advances made to states from the federal unemployment account in the Unemployment Trust Fund.
Document 3: Reduces from 60 to 55 the minimum age for the receipt of military retired pay for non-regular service.

topic.name: Judicial and Educational Legislation
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at improving the efficiency and safety within the judicial system and educational institutions.

Document 1: converts certain temporary U.S. district court judgeships to permanent judgeships
specified U.S. judicial districts
Document 2: requires states to allow a student...to transfer to a safe public school
provide school students, families, and staff with an annual report containing certain school crime statistics
Document 3: directs the President to appoint...one additional circuit judge
reduces from 11 to 8 the number of circuit judges appointed to the District of Columbia circuit court of appeals

topic.name: Veterans and Military Legislation
topic.description: Legislation focused on the rights and benefits of military personnel and veterans in the United States.

Document 1: Designates the Liberty Memorial at the National World War I Museum in Kansas City, Missouri, as the National World War I Memorial.
Document 2: Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to extend for three years after the date of enactment of this Act the annual cap exemption for returning H-2B aliens.
Provides that such amendment shall: (1) take effect as if enacted on December 1, 2008; and (2) apply only to petitions with an approved start date in FY2009-FY2011.
Document 3: Amends the Higher Education Act of 1965 to allow members of the Armed Forces serving on active duty in hostile areas to benefit from the provision barring the accrual of interest on their student loans.
Eliminates the restriction of such benefit on direct consolidation loans to that portion of the loan which is used to repay direct loans.

topic.name: Veterans and Child Welfare Policies
topic.description: This cluster discusses legislative measures aimed at improving the health and welfare of veterans and children affected by substance abuse.

Document 1: establish and operate centers for traumatic brain injury (TBI) research
dissemination throughout the VHA of information produced through the research
Document 2: require a grant-receiving state plan for a child abuse or neglect prevention and treatment program
monitor states to ensure that each one's policies and procedures meet requirements

topic.name: Climate Change Mitigation and Risk Reduction
topic.description: Legislative efforts focused on reducing greenhouse gas emissions and enhancing resilience to natural hazards.

Document 1: establish a carbon incentives program to achieve supplemental greenhouse gas emissions reductions
provide financial incentive payments for activities that increase carbon sequestration
Document 2: Prohibits federal agencies...from imposing a fee or tax on gaseous emissions emitted directly by livestock
Document 3: reauthorize the National Earthquake Hazards Reduction Program
responsible for facilitating the adoption of model building codes

topic.name: U.S. Legislative Initiatives
topic.description: This cluster covers various legislative acts aimed at addressing energy independence, cyber defense, and historical preservation in the United States.

Document 1: Energy Independence Now Act of 2009 - Amends the Internal Revenue Code (IRC)
Coal-to-Liquid Fuel Promotion Act of 2009 - Amends the Energy Policy Act of 2005
Document 2: Cyber Defense National Guard Act - Requires the Director of National Intelligence to report to Congress
logistics of allowing governors to use such a National Guard in states during times of cyber emergency
Document 3: Colonel Charles Young Home Study Act - Directs the Secretary of the Interior to conduct a special resource study
evaluate architectural and archaeological resources of the Home

topic.name: 9/11 Victim Support Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at providing support, health care, and transparency for victims and survivors of the September 11, 2001 terrorist attacks.

Document 1: requires the President to declassify and release to the public the previously redacted portions of the report on the Joint Inquiry into Intelligence Community Activities Before and After the Terrorist Attacks of September 2001
compromise presently ongoing national security operations.
Document 2: establish within the National Institute for Occupational Safety and Health the World Trade Center Health Program
provide medical monitoring and treatment benefits to eligible emergency responders and recovery and cleanup workers who responded to the New York City terrorist attacks on September 11, 2001

topic.name: Health Initiatives for Minority Groups
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures and public health initiatives aimed at improving health outcomes and access to care for minority populations, particularly in relation to HIV/AIDS and other health conditions.

Document 1: grants to health entities for HIV/AIDS education and outreach activities
expand and intensify HIV/AIDS prevention and education activities for minority groups
Document 2: develop a system to collect data on multiple sclerosis (MS)
establish a national surveillance system for the collection and storage of such data
Document 3: authorize the Department of Veterans Affairs (VA) to provide for an operation on a live donor
provide for such an operation at a VA or a non-VA facility

topic.name: Consumer Protection and Transparency Legislation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislation aimed at enhancing consumer rights and transparency in various sectors, including business practices and healthcare.

Document 1: prohibits or restricts a person who is a party to the contract from engaging in written, verbal, or pictorial reviews
bars certain contract provisions that prohibit consumers from commenting publicly about businesses.
Document 2: require states receiving funding under the Edward Byrne Memorial Justice Assistance Grant Program to use an incorporation system that requires applicants for forming corporations to provide and update lists of their beneficial owners
prescribes penalties for providing false or fraudulent beneficial ownership information.
Document 3: allows a tax credit for individuals with health insurance who are ineligible for federal health care
health insurers in the individual market must offer coverage to all individuals.

topic.name: Land Rights and Environmental Policy
topic.description: This cluster discusses legal frameworks related to land rights for Indigenous tribes and environmental regulations concerning fuel emissions.

Document 1: Repeals the Act of May 31, 1918
Gives the Shoshone-Bannock Tribes the exclusive right of first refusal to purchase land
Document 2: Directs the Secretary of the Treasury to disregard certain documents related to coal-fired power generation
Document 3: Amends the Clean Air Act to establish a low-carbon fuel certification and marketing process
Requires reduction of average lifecycle greenhouse gas emissions per unit of energy

topic.name: U.S. Congressional Authority in Military Operations
topic.description: This topic encompasses U.S. policies and legal frameworks governing the deployment of military forces and security agreements, emphasizing the role of Congress in authorizing such actions.

Document 1: U.S. policy that the constitutional authority of Congress shall be protected
any vote of the U.N. Security Council shall only be advisory
Document 2: Prohibits misleading caller identification information with intent to defraud
Document 3: Directs the President to report annually to congressional committees
Prohibits any agreement containing a security commitment from entering into force without congressional approval

topic.name: Legislative Acts and Judicial Authority
topic.description: This topic encompasses various legislative measures that define judicial authority and responsibilities, particularly in relation to specific claims, court proceedings, and public health initiatives.

Document 1: exclusive jurisdiction to the U.S. Court of Federal Claims
The United States is the only entity liable for such a claim
Document 2: Authorizes the presiding judge of a U.S. appellate court
terminate a district court's authority under this Act
Document 3: establish the Agency for Vaccine Safety Evaluation
requires the Director to require vaccine manufacturers to

topic.name: Legislative Acts on Public Policy
topic.description: This cluster encompasses various legislative acts aimed at addressing public safety, immigration, and energy policy issues.

Document 1: Amends the Omnibus Crime Control and Safe Streets Act of 1968 to include a fire police officer as a public safety officer for purposes of death benefits provisions.
Defines a 'fire police officer' as an individual who is serving in accordance with state or local law as a member of a legally organized public safety agency.
Document 2: Allows aliens having E-2 (treaty investor) nonimmigrant status to re-enter the United States after a trip abroad without requesting or obtaining a new visa.
States that there shall be no limit on the number of such re-entries during the period in which the alien lawfully holds such status.
Document 3: Directs the Secretary of Energy to publish a plan to sell light grade petroleum from the Strategic Petroleum Reserve.
Withdraw from such cash proceeds the amount necessary to pay for the direct administrative and operational costs of the sale and acquisition.

topic.name: Health Policy Legislation
topic.description: Legislation focused on improving health care access and treatment for specific conditions and populations.

Document 1: Servicemember Mental Health Review Act
extends the authority of the Physical Disability Board of Review to review disability determinations for mental conditions
Document 2: amends the Clean Air Act to make it unlawful for any person to sell gasoline with less than 10% renewable fuel
Document 3: amends the Internal Revenue Code to exclude from gross income amounts received from the sale of drugs for treating Alzheimer's disease

topic.name: Water Safety and Environmental Protection
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at ensuring the safety of water resources and protecting the environment from contamination and hazards.

Document 1: Amends the Federal Water Pollution Control Act to require the EPA to publish a list of all pathogens and pathogen indicators studied in developing water quality criteria.
Requires coastal recreation water quality monitoring and notification programs to include monitoring consistent with such criteria.
Document 2: Directs the Department of Transportation to conduct a study to determine the economic and environmental risks of oil or hazardous liquids spills in the Great Lakes.
Evaluate the condition and structural integrity of the facilities.

topic.name: Government Oversight and Regulation
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative actions aimed at regulating government operations, including recruitment processes, food safety standards, and security services in conflict zones.

Document 1: Modifies the Coast Guard Academy cadet nomination process
requires that half of each incoming class be composed of cadets nominated by various congressional members.
Document 2: Amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act limit the somatic cell count in all dairy products
Subjects any person that sells, transports, or offers for sale or transport in violation of this Act to a civil penalty.
Document 3: Directs the Secretary of State to ensure that only government personnel provide security services
Requires certain reports to Congress regarding such contracts.

topic.name: Environmental and Security Legislation
topic.description: This cluster discusses U.S. legislation focused on environmental education and international security measures, including counternarcotics and stabilization efforts.

Document 1: No Child Left Inside Act of 2011 - Amends the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965 to require states to develop environmental literacy plans.
Directs the Secretary to award Environmental Education Professional Development Grants to states.
Document 2: Amends the Foreign Assistance Act of 1961 to include a report on interagency counternarcotics strategy for the region.
Prohibits any U.S. government international counternarcotics or anti-crime foreign assistance-related activity from being implemented without the Secretary of State's approval.
Document 3: States that the purpose of this Act is to provide for the development of an expert civilian response capability to carry out stabilization and reconstruction activities.
Authorizes the Secretary, in consultation with the Administrator of USAID, to establish a Response Readiness Corps to provide stabilization and reconstruction activities.
